title: abicheck aggregate report
description: Schema for the JSON document produced by `abicheck aggregate --format json`, the multi-target CI fan-in gate. Three axes are kept separate (ADR-042): gate (each report's own recorded severity decision, combined), coverage (did every required target report?), and compatibility (worst verdict, for reporting). The document is versioned via aggregate_schema_version; additive changes (new optional keys) bump the MINOR component, breaking changes bump the MAJOR. Consumers should accept any document whose MAJOR matches theirs and ignore unknown keys.

aggregate_schema_version.description: SemVer-style MAJOR.MINOR version of this document's structure.

compatibility.description: The reporting-only compatibility axis — NOT the gate.

verdict.description: Worst compatibility verdict over the targets feeding this axis (expected analyzed targets plus gated unexpected ones); null when nothing was analyzed.

analyzed_targets.description: Count of targets contributing to the compatibility verdict.

coverage.description: Did every required expected target actually report?

blocking.description: True when the required-coverage gap contributes to the exit code.

exit_code.description: 0 pass / 1 coverage gap, addition-or-quality block, or a non-verdict per-report failure / 2 source-API break / 4 ABI break.

targets.description: Every expected target, in stable id order — analyzed or unavailable.

unexpected_targets.description: Reports for target ids not in the expected set (a matrix leg the manifest has not caught up to).

reason.description: Present when the target is unavailable — why it could not be analyzed.

gate.description: One target's own recorded CI gate decision.

from_report.description: True when read from the report's own severity/scan gate; false when legacy-derived from the verdict.
